description_long: This is a standard cardboard egg carton designed to hold a dozen eggs, featuring a light brown, slightly textured exterior. The carton has two distinct printed labels on the top: one on the left with a green and black 'YOUR EGGS' logo, and another on the right indicating 'GRADE A LARGE EGGS' in black and yellow. The carton is molded with twelve rounded compartments underneath, each shaped to cradle an egg securely. The surface shows some creases and wear, suggesting it is made from recycled paper pulp. The lid is hinged and can be opened to access the eggs inside, with two small locking tabs visible at the front. The overall appearance is utilitarian, typical of packaging found in grocery stores or kitchens.
description: A light brown cardboard egg carton with printed labels, designed to hold a dozen eggs and featuring molded compartments and a hinged lid.
description_view_0: Front view showing the two printed labels on the lid and the locking tabs at the front edge.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the carton and the edge of the printed labels.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the carton with the molded egg compartments visible underneath.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other edge of the carton and the continuation of the printed labels.
attribute_orientation: lying flat with the lid facing upward
attribute_contextual_placement: Typically used for storing and transporting eggs, often found in kitchens, grocery stores, or refrigerators.
